treatment.label: See Convulsion Fact Sheet, then refer urgently. Keep the child warm.
Refer urgently. Keep the child warm.
See Convulsion Fact Sheet and Hypoglycemia Fact Sheet, then refer urgently. Keep the child warm.
See Hypoglycemia Fact Sheet, then refer urgently. Keep the child warm.

• If the child has no other serious classifications, give fluids for severe dehydration
    OR
  • If the child has another serious classification, transfer to the hospital, the mother giving frequent sips of ORS
  • Advise the mother to continue breastfeeding
  • If there is a cholera epidemic in the area, give Cotrimoxazole
  • If blood in the stool: treat for 3 days with Ciprofloxacin

    • Proceed to the FEVER assessment

• Give more fluids and foods for dehydration (Plan B)
  • Advise the mother to continue breastfeeding
  • Give zinc for 10 days
  • Explain to the mother when to return immediately
  • If blood in the stool: treat for 3 days with Ciprofloxacin
  • Review in 2 days if there is no improvement

    • Proceed to the FEVER assessment

• Providing fluids and food to treat diarrhea at home (Plan A)
  • Give zinc for 10 days
  • Explain to the mother when to return immediately
  • Review in 5 days if there is no improvement

    • Proceed to the FEVER assessment

• Treat for 3 days with ciprofloxacin
  • Explain to the mother when to return immediately
  • See you in 2 days

    • Proceed to the FEVER assessment

• Treat dehydration before transferring. See Dehydration Technical Sheet
  • If the child has another serious classification, transfer to the hospital, the mother giving frequent sips of ORS along the way

    • Proceed to the FEVER assessment

• If blood in the stool: treat for 3 days with Ciprofloxacin
  • Explain to the mother how to feed a child with persistent diarrhea.
  • Give zinc for 10 days and ORS
  • Give multivitamins and minerals
  • See you in 5 days

    • Proceed to the FEVER assessment

• Teach the mother to dry the ear.
  • Dry with wicks 3 times a day until the flow stops.
  • Amoxycillin for 5 days.

    • Proceed to the ANEMIA-PALOUR assessment.

• Teach the mother to dry the ear.
  • Dry the ear using a wick.
  • Give Quinolone ear drops for 14 days.
  • Explain to the mother when to return immediately.
  • See the child again in 5 days.

    • Proceed to the ANEMIA-PALOUR assessment.

• Amoxycillin for 5 days.
  • Paracetamol for 5 days.

    • Proceed to the ANEMIA-PALOUR assessment.

Protective measures (REFER URGENTLY)
Treat the seizure or prevent another seizure with Magnesium Sulfate Inj
ATTENTION to the presentation: 4g in physiological serum IV in 20 minutes
Then 10mg of magnesium sulfate IM with 5g in each buttock
If consciousness does not return: give glucose 50% by slow direct IV (1ml/kg)
See TS Eclampsia

Protective measures (REFER URGENTLY)
If convulsions are in progress: Diazepam 10mg 1 amp IM in adults or 0.5mg/kg diluted in 5% glucose IR in children
If not improved after 15 minutes, give Diazepam again
If the person does not regain consciousness: give glucose 50% by slow direct IV (1ml/kg)
See Technical Sheet Convulsions if child, assess SPT TRA - Profuse sweating and SOI - Intense thirst

Counseling:

  • Treat symptoms (insomnia, headaches, etc.).
  • If signs seriously affect quality of life (very bothersome), transfer to hospital.

To dry with a wick:
  • Roll a clean, absorbent cloth or soft, strong paper into a
    • wick.
  • Place the wick in the ear.
  • Remove the wick when it is damp.
  • Replace the wick with another clean wick and repeat the procedure until the wick is dry.
Never attempt to extract with tweezers! Remove the plug by syringing the ear with a needle-free syringe filled with saline solution (or clean warm water).
